fkcompute.infra — Infrastructure¶

The infra subpackage handles all I/O and external-process concerns: locating and executing the C++ binary, parsing configuration files, and managing intermediate files.

fkcompute.infra.binary¶

Locate and execute the fk_main C++ binary.

Key functions:

  • binary_path() — return the path to the binary (searches PATH first, then the packaged _bin/fk_main).

  • run_fk_binary() — execute the backend as a subprocess.

  • safe_unlink() — remove a file if it exists.

from fkcompute.infra.binary import run_fk_binary

# ilp_file_base = path without .csv extension
# output_base   = path without .json extension
run_fk_binary("data/trefoil_ilp", "data/trefoil", threads=4)

C++ binary execution utilities for FK computation.

This module provides functions for locating and executing the compiled FK computation binary.

fkcompute.infra.binary.binary_path(name: str) → str[source]¶

Find the path to a required binary, checking PATH first then package.

Parameters:

name – Name of the binary (without .exe extension).

Returns:

Path to the binary.

Return type:

str

Raises:

RuntimeError – If the binary cannot be found.

fkcompute.infra.binary.safe_unlink(path: str) → None[source]¶

Safely remove a file, ignoring errors.

Parameters:

path – Path to the file to remove.

fkcompute.infra.binary.run_fk_binary(ilp_file: str, output_file: str, threads: int = 1, verbose: bool = False) → None[source]¶

Run the FK computation binary.

Parameters:

  • ilp_file – Path to the ILP input file (without extension).

  • output_file – Path for the output file.

  • threads – Number of threads to use.

  • verbose – Whether to show binary output.

Raises:

subprocess.CalledProcessError – If the binary execution fails.

fkcompute.infra.config¶

Parse configuration files and braid input strings.

Key functions:

  • load_config_file() — parse a JSON or YAML configuration file into a Python dict.

  • load_inversion_file() — load saved inversion data from a JSON file.

  • load_ilp_file() — load an ILP specification from a CSV file.

  • parse_int_list() — parse a braid string in any supported format.

from fkcompute.infra.config import parse_int_list

parse_int_list("[1,-2,3]")  # [1, -2, 3]
parse_int_list("1,-2,3")    # [1, -2, 3]
parse_int_list("1 -2 3")    # [1, -2, 3]

Configuration file parsing for FK computation.

This module provides functions for loading and parsing configuration files in JSON and YAML formats.

fkcompute.infra.config.load_config_file(config_path: str) → Dict[str, Any][source]¶

Load configuration from JSON or YAML file.

Parameters:

config_path – Path to the configuration file.

Returns:

Parsed configuration data.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If the config file does not exist.

  • ImportError – If YAML file is requested but PyYAML is not installed.

fkcompute.infra.config.parse_int_list(s: str | None) → List[int] | None[source]¶

Parse a string into a list of ints.

Accepts: - JSON-style: “[1, -2, 3]” - Comma-separated: “1,-2,3” - Space-separated: “1 -2 3”

Parameters:

s – String to parse.

Returns:

Parsed list of integers, or None if input is None/empty.

Return type:

list[int] or None

fkcompute.infra.config.load_inversion_file(inversion_file: str) → Dict[str, Any][source]¶

Load inversion data from a JSON file and ensure integer keys.

Parameters:

inversion_file – Path to the inversion data file.

Returns:

Inversion data with integer keys.

Return type:

dict

fkcompute.infra.config.load_ilp_file(ilp_file: str) → str[source]¶

Load ILP data from a file.

Parameters:

ilp_file – Path to the ILP data file.

Returns:

ILP data as a string.

Return type:

str

fkcompute.infra.io¶

File I/O utilities for FK computation.

This module provides utility functions for file operations used throughout the FK computation package.

fkcompute.infra.io.sort_any(xs: List[Any]) → List[Any][source]¶

Sort any list by string representation of elements.

Parameters:

xs – List to sort.

Returns:

Sorted list.

Return type:

list

fkcompute.infra.io.find_where(lst: List[Any], predicate) → Any[source]¶

Find the first element in a list satisfying a predicate.

Parameters:

  • lst – List to search.

  • predicate – Function returning True for the desired element.

Returns:

First matching element, or None if not found.

Return type:

Any

fkcompute.infra.io.find_index(lst: List[Any], predicate) → int[source]¶

Find the index of the first element satisfying a predicate.

Parameters:

  • lst – List to search.

  • predicate – Function returning True for the desired element.

Returns:

Index of first matching element, or None if not found.

Return type:

int

fkcompute.infra.io.csv_to_dicts(csv_file_path: str) → List[Dict[str, str]][source]¶

Read a CSV file into a list of dictionaries.

Supports both plain CSV and gzip-compressed files.

Parameters:

csv_file_path – Path to the CSV file (may end with .gz).

Returns:

List of dictionaries, one per row.

Return type:

list[dict]

fkcompute.infra.io.tsv_to_dicts(tsv_file_path: str) → List[Dict[str, str]][source]¶

Read a TSV file into a list of dictionaries.

Parameters:

tsv_file_path – Path to the TSV file.

Returns:

List of dictionaries, one per row.

Return type:

list[dict]

fkcompute.infra.io.save_dicts_to_tsv(data: List[Dict[str, Any]], file_path: str) → None[source]¶

Save a list of dictionaries to a TSV file.

Parameters:

  • data – List of dictionaries to save.

  • file_path – Path to the output TSV file.
